page_content:
ECON785 Advanced Economics of Natural Resources (3 Credits)

The use of exhaustible and renewable natural resources from normative and positive points of view. Analysis of dynamic resource problems emphasizing energy, mineral, groundwater, forestry, and fishery resources; optimal, equilibrium, and intergenerational models of resource allocation. Prerequisite: Permission of AGNR-Agricultural & Resource Economics department; or (ECON603 and AREC623).

Cross-listed with: AREC785.
